spec_match.rationale: Shows library version-to-version delta evolution — the canonical CDT use case.

interactive.rationale: Version timeline buttons swap source views with diff highlighting.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo now includes adjacent-cache, retained-v1 skip, and skipped-delta fallback scenarios, plus a version-pair compressed byte matrix.

design_system.rationale: Uses color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent-emerald) 15%, transparent) for tinted highlights — rule-#5 compliant.

summary: Verified in chrome-devtools-mcp with Headless Chrome Canary 151 on 2026-06-06: loaded /v130/compression-dictionary-transport-with-shared-brotli-and-shared-zstandard/version-evolution/, selected v2.0.0, toggled the normal previous-version, retained-v1 skip, and skipped-delta fallback scenarios, confirmed the metrics and wire header readouts switch between dcb and Brotli fallback, confirmed the source diff label tracks the advertised dictionary, checked the version-pair byte matrix, and found a clean console with no failed requests. The feature conformance route ran 7/7 assertions passing in the same browser.
